title: Polskie tłumaczenie dokumentu kierunkowego UNESCO dotyczącego ochrony niematerialnego dziedzictwa kulturowego w kontekstach miejskich
content:
Nasza Katedra UNESCO opracowała i udostępnia polskie tłumaczenie dokumentu kierunkowego UNESCO Safeguarding intangible cultural heritage in urban contexts / Ochrona niematerialnego dziedzictwa kulturowego w kontekstach miejskich.

Dokument został zatwierdzony podczas 20. sesji Komitetu Międzyrządowego ds. Ochrony Niematerialnego Dziedzictwa Kulturowego, która odbyła się w Nowym Delhi w dniach 8–12 grudnia 2025 r. Jego celem jest wzmocnienie dialogu między podmiotami zajmującymi się niematerialnym dziedzictwem kulturowym i planowaniem miejskim oraz pogłębienie rozumienia relacji między tymi obszarami.

W dokumencie podkreślono, że żywe dziedzictwo w miastach powinno być rozumiane nie tylko przez pryzmat jego materialnych lub przestrzennych powiązań, lecz także poprzez praktyki, wiedzę, relacje społeczne, sposoby użytkowania przestrzeni oraz potrzeby wspólnot, grup i jednostek, które uznają je za część swojego dziedzictwa. Dokument wskazuje również na znaczenie partycypacyjnych procesów planowania, mapowania żywego dziedzictwa, koordynacji między sektorem kultury i planowania przestrzennego oraz rozpoznawania zarówno korzyści, jak i zagrożeń związanych z urbanizacją.
snippet: Nasza Katedra UNESCO opracowała i udostępnia polskie tłumaczenie dokumentu kierunkowego UNESCO Safeguarding intangible cultural heritage in urban contexts / Ochrona niematerialnego dziedzictwa
creator: admin

title: Chapter by Hanna Schreiber in the #HeritageAlive Special Edition on ICH and Armed Conflicts
content:
We are pleased to share that Hanna Schreiber authored a chapter in the special edition of #HeritageAlive, ICH and Armed Conflicts. Sharing Experiences from the Field.

The chapter, entitled “Intangible Cultural Heritage and the Military. New Partnerships in Uncertain Times”, addresses the role of armed forces in safeguarding intangible cultural heritage in crisis and conflict-related contexts.

Read Chapter: https://www.ichgovernance.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Schreiber_2026-리빙해리티지-단면저해상.pdf

Read the full publication: https://www.ichgovernance.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/2026-리빙해리티지-단면저해상.pdf

In her contribution, Hanna Schreiber reflects on the outcomes of the first-ever conference and workshop in Wrocław, Poland, which brought together military forces, heritage specialists, NGOs and public officials to discuss the safeguarding of intangible cultural heritage in military activities. The event was co-organized by the military International Centre for Training and Research on Cultural Heritage in Danger, the Polish Ministry of Defence, and the UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage in Public and Global Governance at the University of Warsaw.
snippet: We are pleased to share that Hanna Schreiber authored a chapter in the special edition of #HeritageAlive, ICH and Armed Conflicts. Sharing Experiences from the Field.
creator: admin
sourceTitle: UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) in Public and Global Governance

title: Our UNESCO Chair at the 11th session of the General Assembly of the States Parties to the 2003 Convention in Paris
content:
From 17 to 19 June 2026, Hanna Schreiber and Julia Krzesicka-Haberko represented our UNESCO Chair during a series of meetings held at UNESCO Headquarters in Paris in connection with the 11th session of the General Assembly of the States Parties to the Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage.

The visit brought together several complementary dimensions of the 2003 Convention ecosystem: the statutory work of the General Assembly, the civil-society perspective represented by the ICH NGO Forum, the global capacity-building system of the Convention, and academic cooperation within the network of UNESCO Chairs and UNITWIN Networks active in the field of intangible cultural heritage. For us, the meetings offered an important opportunity to contribute to discussions on the future governance of the Convention, the role of knowledge in safeguarding, and the need for more inclusive and accountable forms of cooperation.
snippet: From 17 to 19 June 2026, Hanna Schreiber and Julia Krzesicka-Haberko represented our UNESCO Chair during a series of meetings held at UNESCO Headquarters in Paris in connection with the 11th session

title: Language as a Vehicle of Intangible Cultural Heritage – Summary of the seminar
content:
On 21 May 2026, the UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage in Public and Global Governance at the University of Warsaw co-organised the international seminar “Language as a Vehicle of Intangible Cultural Heritage”, held on the occasion of the World Day for Cultural Diversity for Dialogue and Development. Organised together with the Centre for Research and Practice in Cultural Continuity at the Faculty of “Artes Liberales”, the event brought together researchers, practitioners and heritage experts to discuss linguistic diversity, cultural continuity, heritage governance and the impact of digital technologies and artificial intelligence on living heritage.

Hosted at the Faculty of Political Science and International Studies of the University of Warsaw, the seminar approached language not simply as a communication tool, but as a living cultural infrastructure through which communities transmit memory, ecological knowledge, values and ways of understanding the world. Throughout the discussions, language emerged as inseparable from identity, environment, wellbeing and social relations.
snippet: On 21 May 2026, the UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage in Public and Global Governance at the University of Warsaw co-organised the international seminar “Language as a Vehicle of Intangible

title: Broth as Living Heritage: Supporting the Safeguarding of Culinary Traditions in Poland
content:
Our Chair is pleased to support activities led by Museum of King Jan III’s Palace at Wilanów concerning the documentation and safeguarding of the tradition of rosół (Polish broth soup) as an element of intangible cultural heritage. Prof. Hanna Schreiber is part of the broader expert and partner group involved in the initiative “Tradition of Broth in Poland”, launched by the Museum that aims to support future efforts toward the inclusion of the tradition of preparing and serving rosół on the Polish National List of Intangible Cultural Heritage.

The initiative highlights that rosół is not merely a dish, but also a social and cultural practice deeply embedded in Polish family life, hospitality, care, intergenerational transmission and collective memory. For generations, broth has accompanied family gatherings, celebrations and important life moments, becoming one of the most recognizable elements of Polish culinary culture.
snippet: Our Chair is pleased to support activities led by Museum of King Jan III’s Palace at Wilanów concerning the documentation and safeguarding of the tradition of rosół (Polish broth soup) as an element

title: Save the date: 21 may 2026, international seminar: “Language as a Vehicle of Intangible Cultural Heritage”
content:
We warmly invite you to participate in an international seminar organised by our UNESCO Chair and the Center for Research and Practice in Cultural Continuity at the University of Warsaw.

The event will take place on 21 May 2026 in the Prof. Jan Baszkiewicz Auditorium at the University of Warsaw. The seminar is organised on the occasion of the World Day for Cultural Diversity for Dialogue and Development and will focus on the role of language as a key carrier of intangible cultural heritage.

The programme includes an opening session addressing the significance of language in the context of intangible cultural heritage, followed by two panel discussions:

  • “Minority Languages and Cultural Diversity”
  • “Technology and Artificial Intelligence in Intangible Cultural Heritage”

    • At the end of the seminar, participants will be invited to join a walk concluding with a concert dedicated to Warsaw’s traditional street music. The concert is organised by the Warsaw Intangible Cultural Heritage Team to commemorate the Street Orchestra from Chmielna Street.

    The seminar will be held in English and will take place in person only. A recording will be made available afterwards.
snippet: We warmly invite you to participate in an international seminar organised by our UNESCO Chair and the Center for Research and Practice in Cultural Continuity at the University of Warsaw.
creator: admin
sourceTitle: UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) in Public and Global Governance

title: Premiere of “Wielka Warszawska” at the History Meeting House in Warsaw
content:
On 11 April 2026, the premiere of the documentary “Wielka Warszawska” (2026, 27 min) took place at the History Meeting House (Dom Spotkań z Historią) in Warsaw.

The event was opened by Professor Hanna Schreiber, who introduced the perspective of intangible cultural heritage in Warsaw, referring to the 2003 UNESCO Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage and the work of the Warsaw Intangible Cultural Heritage Team.

“Wielka Warszawska” is an original documentary by Barbara Bossak-Herbst and Mateusz Szlachtycz. The film explores the renowned Warsaw horse racing event, which for years has attracted large audiences from the city and across Poland. It shows how a sporting event can become an important element of a city’s identity.

The film is narrated by Marek Siudym, a film, theatre, and cabaret actor, as well as a sports riding instructor and horse enthusiast. It is available below or on YouTube (also with English subtitles): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kniIZo97FeY
snippet: On 11 April 2026, the premiere of the documentary “Wielka Warszawska” (2026, 27 min) took place at the History Meeting House (Dom Spotkań z Historią) in Warsaw.
creator: admin
sourceTitle: UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) in Public and Global Governance

title: Rethinking Commemoration in Urban Spaces: UNESCO Chair Contribution to the World Cities Culture Forum Exchange
content:
On 13 April 2026, the UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage in Public and Global Governance at the University of Warsaw contributed to the World Cities Culture Forum Leadership Exchange Programme held in Warsaw. The programme, titled “How to Rethink Commemoration in Urban Spaces”, brought together representatives from several cities, including Amsterdam, Kyiv, London, New York, and Warsaw, to exchange perspectives on contemporary approaches to commemoration in urban contexts.

It was organised by the Culture Office of the City of Warsaw (Biuro Kultury m.st. Warszawy), within which operates the Warsaw Intangible Cultural Heritage Team (Zespół ds. Niematerialnego Dziedzictwa Kulturowego Warszawy), responsible for coordinating activities related to intangible cultural heritage at the city level.

As part of the first day of the programme, Professor Hanna Schreiber, UNESCO Chairholder, delivered a lecture titled: Rethinking Commemoration in Urban Space through Intangible Cultural Heritage: A Case of Warsaw.
snippet: On 13 April 2026, the UNESCO Chair on Intangible Cultural Heritage in Public and Global Governance at the University of Warsaw contributed to the World Cities Culture Forum Leadership Exchange

title: Recruitment for the BIP – Kaustinen ICH Academy (Erasmus+)
content:
We are pleased to share the call for applications for the Blended Intensive Programme (BIP) Kaustinen ICH Academy, organized by Centria University of Applied Sciences and the Finnish Folk Music Institute.

The programme includes:

  • 5 days of physical mobility in Kaustinen, Finland (14–18 July 2026),
  • 3 online sessions (before and after the mobility).

    • Participation offers 3 ECTS credits and Erasmus+ mobility funding.

    The programme is held under the auspices of our UNESCO Chair, in connection with the participation of Prof. Hanna Schreiber as a keynote speaker at this year’s ICH Summer Academy in Kaustinen.

    We particularly encourage students interested in:

  • intangible cultural heritage,
  • traditional music and culture,
  • cultural policy,
  • cultural event organization.

    • Application deadline: 20 April 2026
    Submission: SWZMPB, room 309 (Auditorium Building, UW)

    More information: https://wspolpraca.wnpism.uw.edu.pl/2026/04/15/rekrutacja-do-mieszanego-programu-intensywnego-bip-kaustinen-ich-academy-recruitment-for-the-blended-intensive-programme-bip-kaustinen-ich-academy/
snippet: We are pleased to share the call for applications for the Blended Intensive Programme (BIP) Kaustinen ICH Academy, organized by Centria University of Applied Sciences and the Finnish Folk Music
